title: Grand Theft Auto IV

notes:
With Proton 3.16-6, all I had to do was download this dll https://github.com/AccountOneOff/lutris_files/raw/master/xlive.dll and put it in the folder "GTAIV" which you can find in the root directory of the game. That's it, nothing else. You don't have to create any online accounts, just hit "play" button when small window pops up when you launch the game. The game saves locally just fine.

Speaking of game performance, it runs at 30-50 fps with disabled shadows, which is good if you really want to play this game. Tweaking other graphics settings doesn't seem to change anything. Everything is on high (except shadows, of course)

(sorry if there was typos)
title: Grand Theft Auto IV

notes: Download the xlive.dll file https://github.com/AccountOneOff/lutris_files/raw/master/xlive.dll and copy it to ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Grand\ Theft\ Auto\ IV/GTAIV/. Install wine-staging and wine-staging-i386 packeges (e.g. with sudo apt install  wine-staging wine-staging-i386), run this 4 commands: cp /opt/wine-staging/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.16\ Beta/dist/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so, cp /opt/wine-staging/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.16\ Beta/dist/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so, cp /opt/wine-staging/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.16\ Beta/dist/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so and cp /opt/wine-staging/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.16\ Beta/dist/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so. Remember to change Proton\ 3.16\ Beta with your Proton version! Add the line: 127.0.0.1        tv.rockstargames.com into the /etc/hosts file.

notes:
GTA:IV installed via this tutorial: https://pastebin.com/raw/2Y2bVuRi
(The wine downloads have been moved to https://dl.winehq.org/wine-builds/ubuntu/dists/bionic/main/binary-amd64/wine-staging-amd64_3.14.0~bionic_amd64.deb and https://dl.winehq.org/wine-builds/ubuntu/dists/bionic/main/binary-i386/wine-staging-i386_3.13.0-2~bionic_i386.deb)
 
I also use https://github.com/iXit/wine-nine-standalone.

The game is playable. To actually achieve 60 FPS play on minimal settings or try some performance mods. In my case I use a mix of medium and high settings and get between 30 - 35 FPS. However since GTA:IV is very unoptimized ,even on windows, they will sometimes be as high as 50 and sometimes go into the low 10s. 

You can look at some gameplay with the tweaks here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bsDibfanmcg

title: Grand Theft Auto IV

notes: Game works following several fixes outlined in Valve's git issue tracker for Proton. Used xliveless to get the game to start at all, added an entry in /etc/hosts to point tv.rockstargames.com at 127.0.0.1 to get rid of hang after initial menu. Performance and graphical issues are minor with a manual installation of D9VK into the game's WINE prefix. DS4 gamepad works mapped via Steam, although with glitchy prompts switching between keyboard and gamepad bindings onscreen.
title: Grand Theft Auto IV

notes: Had to apply same fixes as on Windows (hosts, xliveless), but otherwise runs great. Played through entire game from start to finish via Proton and D9VK makes performance equal or better to Windows.

title: Grand Theft Auto IV

notes:
Download the xlive.dll 
(https://github.com/AccountOneOff/lutris_files/raw/master/xlive.dll)
and put it in GTA's install folder: 
"~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Grand Theft Auto IV/GTAIV/"

Add the following line to your /etc/hosts file (this redirects the 
connection to rockstar's servers to localhost, effectively blocking the
connection which is necessary for the game to work):
127.0.0.1 tv.rockstargames.com

It disables multiplayer but you can still make LAN games(there's a LAN option somewhere under Multiplayer)

Get d9vk: https://git.froggi.es/joshua/d9vk/-/jobs

in terminal:
cd /home/username/Desktop/build/dxvk-release    (unarchived)
export WINEPREFIX="/home/username/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/12210/pfx"  
./setup_dxvk.sh install

Before d9vk it would run the game but nothing was displayed.

Disabled Esync - it got a bit better but it was ok before as well (PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1)
Enabled Gamemode - I didn notice a change

No big visual glitches here but I played through only the first couple of missions: https://imgur.com/a/wFriZo6
title: Grand Theft Auto IV

notes: This is one of my most played games on Steam. Almost all the time I was playing this game in dual-boot with Windows 10 and after trying it via Proton I can say on Linux this games performance is way better! FPS is way more stable! All in all it is much better experience than running this game on Windows 10. Now some technical things that I did. First of all D9VK is mandatory for this game in my opinion. With DXVK the game was running at 30-40FPS with some visual glitches, while switching to D9VK (0.13f) gives me stable 60FPS (did not check how high it goes without VSync). Even on Windows 10 I could not get stable 60FPS! D9VK and Proton is great combo for this game. Another few tweaks that was done: xliveless xlive.dll, stream.ini changes and launch options to remove limits. You can find a guide how to do these things in other reports. I did nvapi.dll changes too, but I read it was optional while running with D9VK. More than 100 hrs on Windows 10/8 was not that smooth as on Arch Linux with Proton + D9VK! Loving it.
title: Grand Theft Auto IV

notes: First tested without D9VK which required Esync to be disabled (PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1), game was playable but poor performance (~20-30FPS) even with low settings. Second test with D9VK (without PROTON_NO_ESYNC) and FPS skyrocketed to ~60FPS. Both test needed xliveless and also had launch options disable restrictions and edit to allow game to use 4GB memory (https://pastebin.com/raw/2Y2bVuRi).

title: Grand Theft Auto IV

notes: Tried to get this one going a few weeks ago to no avail. Thought I would give it another shot now that Proton 4.11 is out with built-in D9VK support. I'm pleased to say that this seems to have fixed the game for me. When Steam took me through the install process I cancelled the setup for both Windows Live ID and Games for Windows Live, then I dragged the xliveless dll into the game folder. After doing that and enabling D9VK the game worked and got pretty good performance too. The camera is occasionally buggy during cutscenes but the game is perfectly playable.
title: Grand Theft Auto IV

notes:
1. Download the xlive.dll (https://github.com/AccountOneOff/lutris_files/raw/master/xlive.dll) and put it in GTA's install folder: "~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Grand Theft Auto IV/GTAIV/" 

2. Launch options string:  DRI_PRIME=1 %command%   This enables the discrete AMD video card.

Game runs better on Linux than it did in Windows on the same hardware.
title: Grand Theft Auto IV

notes: Works Fine So Far on Old Drivers and Linux Mint! Download the Xlive.dll and you will have no issues!
title: Grand Theft Auto IV

notes: You just need to download xliveless and put it in the game's folder, also you need to use this command for d9vk     PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%       after this the game works but you have to minimize the game, go to your system monitor and kill            "gta4Browser.exe" process and when you go back to the game you will see a big performance improvement especialy if you are cpu bounded. Still i was experiencing a bug with my bluetooth xbox one controller not working properly, but overall the game is still playable with this tweaks.
